verses.text: The father bids his son keep his words as 'the apple of thine eye,' binding them on his fingers and heart, that wisdom may keep him from the strange woman and her flattery. Treasured truth is a guard. Scripture held close shields the heart from seduction.

The father bids his son keep his words as 'the apple of thine eye,' binding them on his fingers and heart, that wisdom may keep him from the strange woman and her flattery. Treasured truth is a guard. Scripture held close shields the heart from seduction.
From his window he watched a young man 'void of understanding' wandering toward her house in the twilight. He courted danger by his very steps. Folly walks knowingly into temptation's path.

From his window he watched a young man 'void of understanding' wandering toward her house in the twilight. He courted danger by his very steps. Folly walks knowingly into temptation's path.

There met him a woman dressed as a harlot, 'subtil of heart,' loud and wandering, lying in wait at every corner. Sin actively hunts the careless. Temptation does not wait to be sought; it stalks the unwary.

There met him a woman dressed as a harlot, 'subtil of heart,' loud and wandering, lying in wait at every corner. Sin actively hunts the careless. Temptation does not wait to be sought; it stalks the unwary.

There met him a woman dressed as a harlot, 'subtil of heart,' loud and wandering, lying in wait at every corner. Sin actively hunts the careless. Temptation does not wait to be sought; it stalks the unwary.

She seized and kissed him boldly, claiming peace offerings paid, and lured him with a perfumed, decked bed. She baited her trap with religion and luxury. The seducer clothes ruin in pleasant and even pious dress.

She seized and kissed him boldly, claiming peace offerings paid, and lured him with a perfumed, decked bed. She baited her trap with religion and luxury. The seducer clothes ruin in pleasant and even pious dress.
'Come, let us take our fill of love,' she said, for her husband was away on a long journey. She promised secrecy and indulgence. Sin whispers that no one will know and no reckoning will come.

'Come, let us take our fill of love,' she said, for her husband was away on a long journey. She promised secrecy and indulgence. Sin whispers that no one will know and no reckoning will come.

With much fair speech she forced him to yield; he followed 'as an ox goeth to the slaughter,' not knowing it was for his life. He went blindly to his doom. The flattered sinner does not see the death awaiting him.

With much fair speech she forced him to yield; he followed 'as an ox goeth to the slaughter,' not knowing it was for his life. He went blindly to his doom. The flattered sinner does not see the death awaiting him.

So the father warns his children to hear and not stray into her ways, for 'her house is the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death.' Strong men have been her victims. The end of the seductress's path is the grave.
